straightforward.definition: Simple and easy to understand, without any hidden meaning.
straightforward.example: The instructions were straightforward and easy to follow.

parenting.definition: The activity of raising and caring for children.

potty-training.definition: Teaching a young child to use the toilet instead of a diaper.

weaning.definition: Gradually stopping breastfeeding and starting to give a baby other food.
weaning.example: She started weaning her baby at six months old.

relationships.definition: The way two or more people are connected and feel about each other.
relationships.example: Strong relationships are built on trust.

connection.definition: A feeling of closeness and understanding between people.
connection.example: Parents need a deep connection with their children.

enhance.definition: To make something better or stronger.

bearing.definition: An influence or effect on something.
bearing.example: Your childhood has a bearing on who you become.

parent.definition: A mother or father who takes care of a child.
parent.example: Every parent wants the best for their child.

tips.example: She gave me some tips on cooking.

tricks.definition: Clever methods to get something done easily.
tricks.example: He knows a few tricks for saving money.

hacks.definition: Quick and clever ways to solve a problem or make life easier.
hacks.example: This kitchen hack saves a lot of time.

upset.definition: To make someone feel sad, worried, or unhappy.

angry.definition: Feeling strong displeasure about something unfair or wrong.
angry.example: He was angry when he heard the lie.

brought.definition: Past tense of 'bring'; here means raised or grown up.
brought.example: She was brought up in a small town.

wish.definition: To want something that is not easy to have or that did not happen.
wish.example: I wish I had studied harder in school.

details.definition: Small, specific pieces of information about something.
details.example: Please give me more details about the plan.
